summary: Requirement.kind is KindDefinition<never>, so skill/hook-keyed requirements don't type-check; widen to string | KindDefinition<any> — coverage resolution needs the kind's identity, not its field type

per.section: requirement — a shipped kind, not a primitive

edit.description: Widen `Requirement.kind` (line 179) from `KindDefinition<never>` to `string | KindDefinition<any>` and update the interface doc: the slot carries only the kind's identity for coverage resolution, never its field type — so a kind whose fields carry required members (skill, hook) assigns.

requirementRows (line 285) reads `requirement.kind?.key`; handle the new `string` arm — a bare string resolves to itself, a definition to its `.key`, mirroring embeddedMemberValue (kind.ts:320-321). RequirementRow.kind stays a string, so no wire/Rust change.

tests.asserts: a requirement keyed to `skill` (a kind with required-member fields) type-checks and emits `kind: "skill"`; a bare-string `kind` is also accepted and emitted verbatim.

acceptance: A requirement can be keyed to skill/hook (or a bare kind-name string) at the keystroke and its RequirementRow emits that identity; `.temper/harness.ts`'s friction-capture requirement can restore its dropped `kind:`; `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Consolidation: mirror the collection child-kind slot `string | KindDefinition<any>` (sdk/src/kind.ts:315, embeddedMemberValue); name it in the commit body. SDK-only, no Rust ripple — RequirementRow.kind already emits a string (declarations.ts:285 reads .key, must handle the string arm). Restoring .temper/harness.ts's dropped friction-capture kind is a follow-on chore(harness), outside build's fence. observed at 3540ebb; scoped at 0aa9e62.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
